title: AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport

The '''AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport''' was an [[Atmosphere/Legends|aerial]] [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] vehicle model.<ref name="Offscreen" /> It featured [[Wing (technology)|wings]], and its enclosed [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] could support a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. The AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport also had a pair of<ref name="Kenner" /> fully rotating<ref name="Offscreen" /> [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]],<ref name="Kenner" /> and the vehicle could place itself in an upright sentinel position. The interior of the vehicle was equipped with [[Viewscreen/Legends|screens]] that displayed [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in outer [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="Offscreen" />

The [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|criminal empire]] of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]],<ref name="Kenner" /> a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] operating out of a [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="Jabba CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|130|Jabba the Hutt (Jabba Desilijic Tiure)}}</ref> used the AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport to defend the structure.<ref name="Kenner" /> One of the Hutt's best pilots, [[Wooof/Legends|Wooof]], occasionally flew an AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport<ref name="Offscreen" /> prior to his<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|334|Wooof}}</ref> and Jabba's [[Death/Legends|deaths]] at the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]]<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In [[1983]], [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] created the AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport for the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (toy line)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' toy line]]. The assembly instructions for the toy depicted the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] operating the vehicle, while it is shown to be used by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] on the packaging.<ref name="Kenner" /> In the [[October 14]], [[2013]] [[StarWars.com]] article "[[Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles]]" by [[Tim Veekhoven]], the depiction of starships in space on screens inside the AST-5 Armored Sentinel Transport was speculated to imply that the vehicle was able to enter a planet's [[Wikipedia:Exosphere|exosphere]] or even travel in space for a short [[Time/Legends|time]] period.<ref name="Offscreen" />
